headline: Cisco and Other Old Tech Stocks Are Hitting New Highs. Is It Time to Worry?
summary: The dot-com bubble began to burst in the spring of 2000, when presidential candidates George Bush and Al Gore emerged from their party conventions, the human genome project unveiled its first draft, and ‘American Beauty’ dominated the Oscars with five awards, including best picture.  More than a quarter-century later, however, those same three stocks are leading an old-tech revival that has powered U.S. markets to a series of record highs, paced by an insatiable appetite for all things related to artificial intelligence.  Investors are seeing few signs of a pullback like the one during the turn of the century crash that rocked tech markets for a decade.

content.title: Cisco and Other Old Tech Stocks Are Hitting New Highs. Is It Time to Worry?
content.summary: The dot-com bubble began to burst in the spring of 2000, when presidential candidates George Bush and Al Gore emerged from their party conventions, the human genome project unveiled its first draft, and ‘American Beauty’ dominated the Oscars with five awards, including best picture.  More than a quarter-century later, however, those same three stocks are leading an old-tech revival that has powered U.S. markets to a series of record highs, paced by an insatiable appetite for all things related to artificial intelligence.  Investors are seeing few signs of a pullback like the one during the turn of the century crash that rocked tech markets for a decade.

headline: AMD and ARM Extend Server Market Gains While Intel Loses Share (AMD)
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) and Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) continued to strengthen their positions in the server processor market during the first quarter of 2026, taking additional share from Intel (NASDAQ:INTC), according to a new report published by UBS. The report also showed that personal computer shipments remained weaker than typical seasonal patterns for a second consecutive quarter.

The report also showed that personal computer shipments remained weaker than typical seasonal patterns for a second consecutive quarter.

headline: Intel Rejoins Formula One Through McLaren Technology Partnership (INTC)
summary: Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) is making a return to Formula One after agreeing to a multi-year partnership with McLaren, where it will serve as the racing organisation’s official compute partner, according to a report from Sky. The partnership is expected to be formally announced on Thursday, although financial details of the agreement have not been disclosed, Sky said.

The partnership is expected to be formally announced on Thursday, although financial details of the agreement have not been disclosed, Sky said.

headline: New report shows AMD and ARM continue server share gains at Intel’s expense
summary: Investing.com -- AMD and ARM continued to gain ground in the server chip market at Intel’s expense in the first quarter of 2026, according to a report from UBS, which also found that PC shipments came in below seasonal norms for the second consecutive quarter.

content.title: New report shows AMD and ARM continue server share gains at Intel’s expense
content.summary: Investing.com -- AMD and ARM continued to gain ground in the server chip market at Intel’s expense in the first quarter of 2026, according to a report from UBS, which also found that PC shipments came in below seasonal norms for the second consecutive quarter.
